title: QA Mapping Spine: Field Structure Theory (FST) / Structural Physics (SP) — Plenum-to-Particle Loop Sequence (v2, axiom-compliant re-map)

pattern_space.name: Plenum configuration space Omega
pattern_space.description: Non-local, isometric loop potential from which space, time, and matter emerge through loop condensation. In this v2 QA mapping, Omega is instantiated as the set of admissible integer STF basis decompositions of first-generation particle loop counts. Continuous-valued quantities such as MeV, frequency, or position are NOT part of Omega; they enter only as outputs of the declared observer projection Pi (see cert bundle). This enforces Theorem NT: continuous functions are observer projections only, never causal inputs to the QA layer. v2 change: v1 allowed MeV comparisons inside the validator without declared firewall; v2 routes all continuous values through Pi exactly once.
primary_claim_refs: Briddell 2026 Chap 15 The Sequences: STF iterations 3, 9, 27, 81, 243, 729, 2187 define the first-generation particle hierarchy.
Briddell 2026 line 861: the proton and neutron are not iterations of the STF although their loop counts are well within the seven iterations.
Briddell 2026 line 795: a fermion mass is composed of six loops, three Rspin real-loop quarks and three Aspin anti-loops quarks.

fields.name: loop_count_total_loves
fields.semantics: Total loop quanta (Loves) in the structure. Integer; non-reducible; lives in the QA discrete layer. A1 compliance: loop counts are strictly positive integers; no zero-loop states are admitted (minimum structure is 3 loops per Briddell's Structor definition).

fields.name: stf_basis_decomposition
fields.semantics: Vector phi = (phi_1, ..., phi_7) of non-negative integer coefficients against the STF basis [3, 9, 27, 81, 243, 729, 2187]. Invariant: loop_count_total_loves == dot(phi, basis). A2 compliance: loop_count_total_loves is a derived invariant computed from phi, never independently assigned.

fields.name: sector_cardinality
fields.semantics: Chiral sector cardinality {Rspin: int, Aspin: int}. For first-generation fermions the invariant is |Rspin| == |Aspin| == 3 per Briddell line 795. Integer-valued; no float.

fields.name: deployment
fields.semantics: Deployment state for each sector. Does not change loop count; acts on observer-relative visibility only via cloaking logic.

fields.name: cloak_state
fields.semantics: Observer-relative visibility obstruction. Briddell: any particle that cannot be seen is an Aspin particle. Does not affect loop count or STF decomposition. S2 compliance: all fields are int or enum; no float state.

generators.name: sigma
generators.semantics: Add one loop quantum (one Love). Integer-valued transformation. Preserves A1 (loop count stays positive integer).

generators.name: lambda_3
generators.semantics: STF iteration scale step. Promotes an iteration-k configuration to iteration-(k+1). Integer multiplication by 3; preserves A1.

generators.name: mu
generators.semantics: Chirality involution. Identity on loop count; permutes sector cardinality.

generators.name: kappa
generators.semantics: Deployment-state toggle. Identity on loop count; changes observability only. Briddell: condensed Aspin loopage forms the interaction boundary.

generators.name: chi
generators.semantics: Cloaking operator. Modifies cloak_state to produce CLOAKED_FROM_OBSERVER obstructions. Identity on loop count and STF decomposition. T1 compliance: generator application is path-length only; no continuous time.

invariants.name: l_total
invariants.description: Total loop count. Preserved by mu, kappa, chi. Changed by sigma (+1) and lambda_3 (x3) per their explicit signatures.

invariants.name: phi_stf
invariants.description: STF basis coefficients satisfying dot(phi, [3,9,27,81,243,729,2187]) == l_total. Always integer; derived per A2 (never independently assigned).

invariants.name: sector_balance
invariants.description: For first-generation fermions: Rspin cardinality equals Aspin cardinality equals 3 (Briddell line 795). Type-level invariant enforced by ENFORCE_SECTOR_INVARIANT validator step.

obstruction_types.rule: If a declared STF basis decomposition does not sum to the declared l_total under integer arithmetic (dot(phi, basis) != l_total).
If a first-generation fermion state packet has Rspin cardinality not equal to Aspin cardinality, or either is not exactly 3.
If cloak_state blocks boundary intersection for the declared observer sector. Briddell: any particle that cannot be seen is an Aspin particle.
Observer-layer WARNING ONLY. If Pi(l) differs from a PDG reference value by more than a declared tolerance. Does not invalidate the underlying integer claims. Severity is warning.

If the primary source contains arithmetic or bookkeeping slips (typos) that the completion layer has had to correct to maintain STF consistency. Logged for corrigendum, not promoted to validation failure. Severity is warning.

determinism.canonical_json: UTF-8, keys sorted, separators comma colon, no NaN or Infinity, numbers as JSON numbers. Inherited from qa_cert_core.canonical_json_compact.

steps.action: Load spine and certificate JSON; verify schema_version matches QA_MAP_MODULE_SPINE.v1 and QA_CERT_BUNDLE.v1 respectively; verify cross-reference of module_id.
Compute canonical_json and sha256 for both artifacts; compare with manifest if provided.
For each integer-arithmetic claim (proton partition, lambda decay bookkeeping): verify sum(phi_i * basis_i) == l_total and that any declared subtraction arithmetic is exact in integer.
For first-generation fermion claims: verify Rspin cardinality equals Aspin cardinality equals 3 as a type-level invariant.

For the calibration postulate cert: apply observer projection Pi to each declared STF iteration and compute drift against PDG reference values. All float arithmetic is explicitly observer-layer and logged, never fed back to state. Theorem NT firewall crossed exactly once at this step per claim.
Emit {move, fail_type, invariant_diff} records for any violated integer check. Log calibration drifts under CALIBRATION_DRIFT warning. Log in-source typos under SOURCE_INTERNAL_INCONSISTENCY warning.
Return pass/fail plus metrics bundle: partition sums, Pi values, drift values, and the full calibration table for transparency.

cover_letter_claims: Twelve-page manuscript introducing Structural Physics (SP) as an application of Field Structure Theory; target section Nuclear Physics; manuscript ID 1850870 submitted 2026-04-08.
Maps STF loop counts onto first-generation particle masses via the declared unit-identification postulate P1 (1 Love equals one electron rest mass).

Original work; not under consideration elsewhere. Companion paper to Briddell (2026) The Plenum-to-Particle Loop Sequence of Field Structure Theory, providing deterministic machine-checkable verification of its structural claims without proposing new physics.
